Company Description

Our collaborative culture sets us apart. We help experts become true specialists and provide the long-term structure and platform to accelerate their success.

Job Description

Colliers is seeking an experienced technology professional to manage and deliver the application, data, and analytics solutions that support our Occupier Services across Australia.

This role is focused on the successful implementation and ongoing management of core technology platforms. You will be responsible for executing technology projects, delivering robust data solutions, and ensuring our client-facing teams and their clients get maximum value from our systems.

You will work closely with our Enterprise Clients teams, business stakeholders, and global Colliers technology functions to manage and enhance our technology offerings, ensuring they are operationally sound and effectively delivered.

Your responsibilities will include:

Lead technology projects, system upgrades, and new platform rollouts.
Manage day‑to‑day performance of key lease, portfolio, and transaction systems.
Manage BI and analytics outputs, including dashboards, reporting, and hands‑on data work using Excel, SQL, and BI tools.
Support business development by providing platform expertise, demonstrations, and input into RFPs and proposals.
Drive solution design, client onboarding, system adoption, and ongoing technology support.
Qualifications
8–12+ years’ experience in technology, data, systems, or analytics roles.
Bachelor’s degree in IT, Computer Science, Data, Engineering, Business, or a related discipline.
Experience in real estate, property, or professional services preferred.
Demonstrated experience supporting client-facing teams and commercial processes.
Experience working with regional or global technology teams.

Technical Capability

Advanced Microsoft Excel skills.
Strong working knowledge of SQL and relational databases.
Solid understanding of the Microsoft ecosystem (Microsoft 365, Power BI; Azure concepts desirable).
Additional Information

We foster an environment in which everyone at Colliers feels respected and comfortable bringing their authentic selves to do their best work. It’s our people who set us apart and we actively recruit people who represent the diversity of the communities in which we operate. We take great pride in doing what’s right and operating in ways that benefit the business, our people, our clients, our communities, and the environment.

Experts join experts, and we welcome you to join us as we lead the industry into the future.

Please be advised that applications will only be accepted directly rather than via recruitment agencies.

Ad Text:
Company description:



Insignia Financial Limited is an Australian financial services company which offers a range of products and services including financial advice, superannuation, investment management and trustee services



Job description:



Senior Risk and Governance Specialist

Make risk real and help us make better decisions
Put your fingerprints on the future to progress your career
Embed risk that enables safe and trusted acceleration of Technology

The opportunity to join our team

We're looking for a Senior Risk and Governance Specialist to join our 1st Line Technology Risk team and play a key role in shaping how technology and cloud risk is managed across Insignia Financial. In this role you'll partner closely with a key business unit as its applications transition into and operate within cloud environments, helping to identify, assess and manage technology risks while helping teams make confident, well-informed decisions.

You'll work closely with teams who are actively building and operating in the cloud, gaining exposure to multiple cloud technologies and cross-functional teams.

The cloud environment is still maturing, which means your work will have real impact. You'll help shape how technology risk is managed in a cloud-first organisation, guiding teams through new challenges and strengthening controls as the environment evolves. On top of that, you'll be part of a close, collaborative team that values support, open communication, and a bit of fun along the way.

This is a hands-on, first line technology risk role giving you the opportunity to influence how risk is managed as the organisation continues its cloud-first journey.

Key Responsibilities:

Act as the first-line Technology Risk partner to a dedicated technology business unit
Support risk identification, assessment and remediation across cloud-hosted applications
Provide pragmatic guidance on technology and cloud risk matters
Work closely with cloud platform teams
Manage and track technology incidents, issues and remediation actions
Contribute to technology risk uplift initiatives and governance improvements

What you will bring

You're someone who understands technology risk but is equally comfortable engaging with technical teams. You enjoy working closely with stakeholders, asking the right questions, and helping teams think through risk in a practical way rather than simply applying frameworks.

Strong experience in first-line Technology Risk
Exposure to cloud environments, ideally AWS (Azure or GCP also valuable)
Ability to engage confidently with technical teams and stakeholders
Understanding of technology risk frameworks and governance practices
Knowledge of the Australian regulatory landscape (APRA CPS 234, CPS 230 etc.)
Strong communication and stakeholder engagement skills

Find the better way

What gets us excited about working here? Helping people feel good about their money as one of Australia's leading financial wellbeing organisations. From those starting out to those retiring. As your team, we'll show up for you and together we will create financial wellbeing for every Australian. Because we know people who are confident about their financial future live better lives.

If this sounds like your kind of career, you sound like our kind of person. With us, you'll do work that builds your technical know-how and challenges our entire industry to move forward. Along the way, you'll be free to explore new ideas and technology, solve problems in a team, and independently to get great things done. Around here, we like to call it "putting our fingerprints on the future".

People-inspired tech

Deep expertise combined with creativity can take on the trickiest of problems. Join our team of brilliant minds and mentors as we explore our way to innovation and apply technology in more human ways than ever.

To Apply

Read more here about why you should join our team.

Applicants will be required to provide evidence of their eligibility to work in Australia, and at a minimum be required to undertake police and basic credit checks as a condition of employment.

Please note that applications from agencies will not be considered at this time.

We acknowledge and celebrate the richness that individual differences bring to our team. If you need assistance or an adjustment during the application process, please reach out and let us know.

Job Title: Director, Education and Workforce Policy, Asia
Ad Text:
Overview


We are looking for a Director, Education and Workforce Policy focused on the Asia region to advance policy and translate it into practice to support students, teachers, families, and workers in the age of AI.

This person will join the Global Education and Workforce Policy team in Microsoft Elevate to steer impactful initiatives across the Asia region. Acting as a key external representative for Microsoft, you’ll engage with regional and national officials, build meaningful relationships, and champion strategies that reflect Microsoft’s core values while advancing important policy and business goals. You will help guide Microsoft’s positions on critical education and workforce topics—such as the benefits and risks of AI, the impact of AI on work, responsible AI adoption, and how to ensure all citizens develop foundational AI literacy.  

As Director, Education and Workforce Policy, you will collaborate closely with Microsoft Global Affairs in the Asia region, Elevate AI Skills Directors, and Microsoft’s sales organization to drive policy and the adoption of Microsoft’s products, services, and solutions among government and education clients. This high-impact position also interacts with senior Microsoft leaders and executives, as well as a broad range of internal and external audiences, amplifying Microsoft’s voice and advancing strategic goals. This role reports to the General Manager, Global Education and Workforce Policy.   

Microsoft’s mission is to empower every person and every organization on the planet to achieve more. As employees we come together with a growth mindset, innovate to empower others, and collaborate to realize our shared goals. Each day we build on our values of respect, integrity, and accountability to create a culture of inclusion where everyone can thrive at work and beyond. 

Responsibilities

Strategy & Program Leadership 

Execute the global education and workforce policy strategy through high-impact advocacy and programs in the Asia region, especially in target countries and localities.

Lead the design and execution of key policy campaigns, collaborating across functions regionally and globally to amplify Microsoft’s voice. 

Policy Development & Advocacy 

Lead regional policy analysis and recommendations on a diverse set of education and workforce issues, with a focus on AI.

Localize the global education and workforce policy agenda to a region and country’s needs and political environment.

Support adoption of AI literacy frameworks, standards, and guidance across education systems (K–12, vocational, higher education, and lifelong learning).

Write policy briefs and responses to government requests for input.

Anticipate emerging opportunities and threats and lead the development of forward-thinking strategies to address them. 

Stakeholder Engagement 

Create coalitions or work with a diverse range of partners—from government agencies to think tanks and community leaders—aligning interests and turning stakeholders into advocates for Microsoft’s policy and business goals.

Cultivate and sustain influential relationships with regional institutions, ministry staff, regulators, and opinion leaders, building Microsoft’s reputation as a trusted partner and thought leader.

Convene and influence key stakeholders, including ministries, employers, labor unions, civil society, and workforce intermediaries. 

Strengthen public-private partnerships with government, including contributions to workforce hubs, advisory councils, and national digital skills initiatives. 

Subject Matter Expertise & Influence 

Advise and collaborate with leaders to secure buy-in on policy positions and prepare leaders for public testimony on critical and complex issues.

Develop national and regional workforce narratives, strategic memos, and policy briefs for senior leadership.  

Prepare executive materials for engagements, external meetings, parliamentary preparation, and strategy sessions.  

Author policy analysis and feedback that summarizes and shapes national and regional education and workforce ecosystems.

Promote responsible AI in education principles across internal and external education and workforce programs. 

Internal Collaboration 

Partner with regional and local Microsoft teams by providing policy analysis, insight briefs, recommendations, and meeting preparation.  

Engage regularly with regional government affairs teams to align country-level actions with global priorities.   

Lead and collaborate on high-impact, cross-group projects to develop actionable, scalable solutions for Microsoft’s education and workforce priorities.

Provide thought leadership to overcome regulatory hurdles, partnering closely with the Public Sector and key business leaders to unlock new market opportunities.

Open doors with policymakers, ministries, and institutions to advance Microsoft’s AI capacity-building solutions.  

Coordinate communications across departments to guarantee all stakeholders stay informed and engaged on evolving issues. 

Qualifications

Required Qualifications

Bachelor’s Degree in Public Policy, Education Policy, Technology Policy, or related field AND plus 6+ years of experience in government affairs, corporate affairs, public policy, or government, or related area.

OR equivalent experience. 

A track record of building and managing strategic relationships to champion education and/or workforce policy priorities.

Demonstrated expertise in AI literacy, responsible AI in education, or digital skills policy.

Preferred Qualifications

8+ years of experience working with government ministries, regional/global institutions, unions, or large cross-sector alliances.   

Familiarity with emerging technology policy issues, or the ability to quickly master complex technical and regulatory topics.

Experience crafting responses to legislative and policy processes.

Exceptional political awareness, analytical ability, and government relations expertise.

Strong writing, analysis, communication, and project management skills.

Ability to work independently, manage complex workstreams, and build trust with senior stakeholders.

Ability to navigate ambiguity in a fast-paced environment and contribute across a wide range of policy activities. 

#CELA

This position will be open for a minimum of 5 days, with applications accepted on an ongoing basis until the position is filled.

Microsoft is an equal opportunity employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to age, ancestry, citizenship, color, family or medical care leave, gender identity or expression, genetic information, immigration status, marital status, medical condition, national origin, physical or mental disability, political affiliation, protected veteran or military status, race, ethnicity, religion, sex (including pregnancy), sexual orientation, or any other characteristic protected by applicable local laws, regulations and ordinances. If you need assistance with religious accommodations and/or a reasonable accommodation due to a disability during the application process, read more about requesting accommodations.

Head of Quantitative
B2B Financial Services Technology | Global Scale | Sydney (Hybrid)

Greenfield opportunity to define a global quantitative capability


Direct influence on product strategy and international expansion


Work with sophisticated institutional clients at scale


A high-growth B2B financial services technology company is appointing a Head of Quantitative in a newly created role to build and lead its next-generation modelling capability.

Operating at the intersection of wealth management, actuarial science and enterprise software, this business partners with tier-one financial institutions across Australia, the UK and Europe to modernise how financial advice is delivered at scale.

This is a rare opportunity to architect stochastic modelling frameworks from first principles, while building and leading a world-class quantitative function with global reach.

The opportunity
The business is at a pivotal inflection point and is poised for continued market traction.

Having successfully deployed deterministic advice models at institutional scale, the next phase of growth requires the introduction of sophisticated stochastic modelling to unlock new markets, new products and materially improved customer outcomes.

As Head of Quantitative, you will:

Define the quantitative strategy


Lead the technical uplift to stochastic modelling


Build and scale a high-performing team


Act as the ultimate authority on model risk, integrity and regulatory alignment
This is a genuine player–coach role, with significant hands-on technical ownership.

What you’ll be responsible for
Architect and deliver stochastic modelling frameworks (Monte Carlo, scenario generation, multi-goal optimisation)


Lead the transition from deterministic Excel-based models to scalable calculation engines


Translate complex regulatory and advice policy requirements into robust quantitative logic


Build, mentor and scale a quantitative team across Australia and global markets


Partner closely with Product and Engineering to productionise models at scale


Engage directly with tier-one institutional clients, actuarial teams and regulators


Own quantitative governance, validation and model risk standards globally
What it will take to make an impact
Deep expertise in stochastic / actuarial / quantitative modelling


Strong hands-on background in tools such as Excel, Python or actuarial modelling platforms


Experience operating in regulated financial services environments (wealth, retirement, insurance or advice technology)


Proven ability to lead technical teams while remaining hands-on


Confidence engaging with C-level stakeholders, institutional clients and regulators


Comfort operating in ambiguity within a high-growth, product-led business


Advanced qualifications in Actuarial Science, Mathematics, Statistics or a related quantitative field are highly regarded.

If you’re motivated by complex problems, real-world impact and global scale, this is a rare leadership opportunity.  Call Michelle Rubinstein at The Onset on 0413 463 043 for a confidential discussion or apply online.

Mascot, Sydney | 12-Month Contract | Major Enterprise Transformation




Introduction

Our client is seeking a highly experienced Change Manager to play a critical role in a major enterprise Data & AI transformation program within one of Australia’s most recognised organisations.

This role will lead Organisational Change and Planning for a large-scale Data Modernisation program, establishing a modern AI-enabled data platform designed to support advanced analytics, machine learning and enterprise-wide data democratisation.

Working with senior stakeholders across technology, operations and business units, you will drive adoption of new platforms, operating models and governance frameworks that will enable the organisation to become a truly data-driven enterprise.

This is a 12-month engagement based in Mascot HQ, supporting a multi-million-dollar strategic program aligned with the organisation’s broader AI and digital transformation strategy.


About 9X5 Consulting

We are transforming the way business and technology is managed by putting real-time data into the hands of every decision maker across organisations. The insight garnered from diverse backgrounds, perspectives and lived experiences results in pioneering innovations across the organisation and better experiences for our customers. The more diverse our talent, the more impact we have on each other and on our valued clients.

Our clients range from small SME's right through to some of the largest ASX listed organisations in Australia, so experience at selling to stakeholders at this level is required.


A bit about you

You have deep experience delivering enterprise change within complex transformation programs

You thrive working with senior stakeholders including executive leadership

You understand how to drive adoption of data, analytics and digital platforms

You are highly skilled in stakeholder engagement, influence and communication

You are comfortable navigating large organisations with multiple business units

You enjoy helping organisations transition from traditional operating models to data-driven decision making


Key Responsibilities                           

Lead and execute the change strategy and planning strategy for a large-scale data modernisation program

Conduct change impact assessments and readiness evaluations across business units

Drive stakeholder engagement across executive, operational and technical teams

Develop and deliver communication strategies and change artefacts

Support the rollout and adoption of data platforms, analytics capabilities and governance frameworks

Design and deliver training and enablement programs for analysts, business users and technical teams

Monitor change readiness, adoption metrics and behavioural transformation

Identify and mitigate organisational risks and resistance to change

Collaborate closely with program leadership, product teams and delivery partners

Ensure successful transition of initiatives into business-as-usual operations




Job Requirements

This role requires a senior Change Manager with extensive experience delivering organisational change within large-scale technology or data transformation programs in complex enterprise environments.

You will be comfortable operating in high-profile transformation programs, engaging senior stakeholders and driving adoption of new ways of working across large organisations.

Essential

Minimum 10+ years experience in organisational change management

Experience delivering change within large enterprise transformation programs

Proven ability to lead change initiatives within technology, data or digital transformations

Experience working with executive stakeholders and senior leadership teams

Strong experience with stakeholder engagement and change communications

Demonstrated experience conducting change impact assessments and readiness planning

Ability to operate within Agile, Lean or hybrid delivery environments

Experience developing training and capability uplift programs

Strong understanding of organisational risk and change management frameworks

Excellent communication, facilitation and influencing skills

Desirable

Experience working within data platform, analytics or AI transformation programs

Experience within large operational or complex workforce environments

Familiarity with data governance frameworks, metadata catalogues or data product models

Experience supporting self-service analytics or data democratisation initiatives

Previous experience within aviation industry or in the transport, logistics or similar operational industries

Personal attributes

Strategic thinker who can translate complex change into practical outcomes

Strong communicator capable of influencing across technical and non-technical audiences

Collaborative and able to work effectively within large multi-disciplinary teams

Highly organised and capable of managing multiple workstreams simultaneously

Resilient and adaptable in large, fast-moving transformation environments

Passionate about helping organisations become data-driven and insight-led

Deal-breakers (stuff we don’t want in a new team member)

A “that’ll do” approach to our clients’ projects;

A closed mind to change;

Big egos. We’re really, really not keen on big egos here.




If you enjoy helping organisations navigate large-scale transformation and take pride in guiding people through complex change, this is an excellent opportunity to play a key role in shaping the adoption of modern data and AI capabilities within a large, enterprise environment.


*Only applicants with citizenship, permanent visa will be considered.

No agencies please!

Our organisation brings experts together to create national and international standards that serve as essential guidelines for products and services, ultimately benefiting the Australian community. As the Capital & Liquidity Analyst Lead, you will be a key member of the Financial Analytics team within the Finance & Data Division. You will hold Line 1 accountability for capital, liquidity and balance sheet risk management activities and provide forward‑looking insights that support informed decision making across the Bank.

Your key responsibilities will include:

Lead the development and delivery of the Bank’s ICAAP and Recovery Plan, ensuring alignment with APRA prudential standards and regulatory expectations.

Design and deliver enterprise-wide stress testing frameworks, including scenario design, model development and interpretation of outcomes to inform capital planning and strategic decision making.

Present capital and liquidity insights to Executive Committees and the Board, supporting strategic balance sheet decisions.

Develop and maintain liquidity analysis and metrics, including Liquidity Coverage Ratio (LCR) capability.

Ensuring strong governance, documentation and alignment across capital, liquidity, ALM, FTP and forecasting frameworks.

Providing technical leadership, coaching and knowledge sharing within the Financial Analytics team.

About you

To be successful in this role, you will bring strong technical capability, sound judgement and the ability to influence and engage senior stakeholders.

You will also demonstrate:

Significant experience in financial institutions, with strong knowledge of balance sheet management, capital, liquidity and financial risk management.

Proven experience leading ICAAP, capital management, stress testing or balance sheet risk frameworks within a regulated financial institution.

The ability to design, interpret and explain complex financial models, stress testing outcomes and risk frameworks.

Strong analytical skills, attention to detail and the ability to translate technical analysis into clear insights for non‑technical audiences.

Well developed communication and stakeholder engagement skills, with confidence working with Executives and senior leaders.

A proactive, risk‑aware mindset and commitment to continuous improvement and professional development.

Qualifications

Tertiary qualification in Finance, Business, Economics, Actuarial Studies or a related discipline is essential.

Postgraduate or professional qualifications (CA, CPA, CFA or similar) and relevant industry memberships are desirable.

A workplace for you to be you. 

Come and work with a team that values your unique perspective, contribution, career development and growth. 

Why you’ll love working with us:

People First Bank – our new brand – says exactly what we’re all about: people. We're committed to supporting and growing our team and creating an outstanding work environment.

You’ll have access to financial and lifestyle benefits to support your success and individuality:

Flexibility and hybrid working arrangements.

Extend your salary with discounts on banking, health insurance, and shopping across Australia.

Advance your career with training, study support, and project opportunities.

Competitive leave benefits that empower employees to take time for themselves and loved ones.

Wellbeing support with our Employee Assistance Program, wellbeing leave, mental health app, and mental health ambassadors’ program.

Make a difference with paid volunteering, Workplace Giving, and diverse community initiatives.

Be recognised for your contributions through our peer-driven recognition program.

A bit about People First Bank: 

In March 2023, People’s Choice and Heritage Bank merged to form Heritage and People’s Choice Limited - Australia's leading mutual bank.  We are now on an exciting journey to bring together our products, services, and technology under a single brand - People First Bank.

We have over 700,000 members and 1,900 employees, with dual head offices in Adelaide and Toowoomba, an Australian-based contact centre and branches across five states. 

With a vision ‘To deliver positive change through banking’, People First Bank serves members and contributes to the community, while offering an inclusive, flexible work environment. We’re big enough to make an impact; but agile enough to influence and do interesting work.

As a Senior Business Analyst, you will play a key role in shaping business strategy and delivering enterprise-aligned solutions within the GBIS PMO team. This full-time, 12 month fixed-term opportunity (hybrid, with three days in the office) is ideal for seasoned professionals who excel at solving complex business problems, influencing senior stakeholders, and driving transformative change.

You’ll be part of a collaborative team based in Collins Street, contributing to strategic programs and mentoring other analysts to build organisational capability.

Please note this role is only open to Australian Citizens.




About the role 

Lead requirements gathering for large, complex, multi-stakeholder programs across business and technology domains. 

Develop business cases and define enterprise-level requirements, ensuring full traceability through design, build, testing, and deployment. 

Conduct AS IS and TO BE process analysis across multiple business units using BPMN 2.0 standards. 

Collaborate with architects, solution designers, finance, and operational leaders to shape strategic solutions aligned with organisational and client outcomes. 

Work closely with finance teams to ensure clear understanding of end-to-end finance and commercial processes including procurement, client billing, contract variations, and cost management. 

Act as a trusted advisor to executives, influencing priorities and scope. 

Mentor junior and mid-level analysts, promoting consistency in methods and tools. 

Contribute to governance forums and track benefits realisation linked to BA deliverables. 

Drive alignment of business requirements with enterprise architecture and future-state capabilities. 




Skills and experience 

Strategic thinker with strong analytical and problem-solving skills. 

Must have Australian Citizenship. 

Deep experience in utilities, transport, telecommunications, or other asset-intensive and field-operations industries. 

Must have experience with implementation or optimisation of Field Service Management systems. 

Strong understanding of finance and commercial processes, including procurement, work order management, billing cycles, invoicing, and revenue recognition. 

Advanced facilitation, negotiation, and conflict management abilities. 

Excellent communication and stakeholder engagement skills, including executive-level relationships. 

Ability to work autonomously while thriving in fast-paced and collaborative environments. 

Leadership and mentoring capability with a focus on building BA practice maturity and consistent analysis standards. 




Experience: 

7+ years in business analysis with proven success in IT environments. 

Extensive experience in business process modelling (BPMN 2.0) and enterprise-level analysis. 

Strong background in ERP or finance-related programs and large-scale implementations. 

Proficiency in tools such as Visio, JIRA, Confluence, and advanced data analysis methods. 

Exposure to Agile frameworks and ability to guide teams in Agile practices. 




About Us 

Service Stream is an equal opportunity ASX-listed business that develops and operates Australia's essential services networks across telecommunications, utilities, transport, defence, and social infrastructure industries. We keep communities connected. 




Benefits of working with us include: 

A safe and supportive culture that values diversity and inclusion. 

Flexible hybrid working arrangements. 

Genuine career development and advancement opportunities. 

Access to special employee benefits and discounts. 

Various paid leave options – parental, cultural, community service, study, corporate volunteering, and purchased leave. 




How to apply 

Please submit your interest with the Apply button where you will be directed to create a profile on our system. We celebrate diversity at Service Stream and welcome applications from Aboriginal and/or Torres Strait Islander peoples, people with a disability or neurodivergence, and those from LGBTIQA+, Veteran or other diverse groups. 
Applications will not be accepted via email. If you require any reasonable adjustments to the recruitment process, please email talent.corporate@servicestream.com.au.

Job Title: Senior Financial Planning and Analytics (Business Partner)
Ad Text:
A senior finance leadership opportunity is available with our client, a large and complex institution operating in the education sector in Canberra. This appointment is being offered as a maternity leave cover and is expected to continue until June 2027. A competitive total remuneration package is available.

This role exists to provide end-to-end financial leadership and commercial support across a broad operating environment. The successful candidate will help shape decision-making by bringing together financial control, planning, modelling and commercial insight. They will work closely with senior stakeholders, translating complex financial information into practical advice that supports both day-to-day operations and longer-term objectives.

The position also carries people leadership responsibility. It will lead a finance team spanning management, analyst and processing capability, while working across a wide stakeholder group to improve performance, strengthen processes and ensure high quality financial support.

What the role will focus on

Leading financial planning, forecasting, reporting and analysis across the function
• Ensuring financial control processes are effective and aligned with broader organisational requirements
• Advising senior leaders on commercial decisions, strategic priorities and emerging risks
• Supporting business case development and objective review with sound commercial judgement
• Building trusted relationships across a diverse stakeholder environment, including external partners
• Strengthening finance service delivery through clear leadership and continuous improvement
What success looks like
The person in this role will provide reliable, well-judged financial and commercial advice in a complex setting. They will support senior stakeholders with clarity and credibility, keep financial operations running effectively and lead a capable team that delivers accurate, timely and practical support.

Required experience and skills

Relevant tertiary qualifications plus CA or CPA, or equivalent
• Extensive senior level experience across financial management, control, commercial management and modelling
• Strong capability in insight driven analysis and turning that analysis into practical recommendations
• Advanced communication skills, with the judgement to adapt messages for different audiences
• Proven stakeholder management experience, including influencing and negotiation at senior level
• Demonstrated leadership capability in complex organisations
• Strong technology skills, including advanced Excel and exposure to enterprise systems
Benefits

Fixed term senior appointment, 12-18 months
• Competitive total remuneration package
Pre employment checks will be required before commencement. The brief refers to standard verification and screening requirements.
To discuss this opportunity confidentially, contact sarah.purdue@bluefinresources.com.au

Category:
Product Management & Development (Information & Communication Technology)
Government - Federal (Government & Defence)
Ad Text:
About the ABC 

The ABC is the nation's most trusted and independent source of Australian conversations, culture, and stories. With over 4,000 employees from diverse backgrounds across over 50 locations around Australia and overseas, we are proud to create, curate and deliver high-quality content that informs, educates and entertains Australian communities 

About the Role 

As Product Manager, Audience Reengagement you will be responsible for shaping product strategy and delivery, while driving technology and capability enhancements across key ABC marketing, media and related audience reengagement tools and platforms. 

Working within a cross-functional team you will lead the technical management and optimisation of the ABC’s Marketing Automation Platform and CRM capabilities, within the broader ABC technology ecosystem of data, personalisation, recommendations, identity and audience facing products. You will contribute to roadmap development and be responsible for translating priorities into clear, actionable backlogs and delivery plans.  

You will work closely with Marketing, Content and Data stakeholders to identify opportunities to improve audience engagement and retention, ensuring initiatives are informed by data and aligned to organisational objectives. This includes supporting experimentation, defining success metrics and contributing to performance reporting. You will provide technical subject matter leadership and guidance to ensure platforms achieve privacy, cyber security and technology best practice, while supporting activities around platform monitoring and technical resolution of incidents and issues. 

 In this role you will help ensure strong delivery discipline across planning cycles, maintain attention to detail in platform implementation and support governance and privacy standards. You will also contribute to improving ways of working across product and engineering to ensure sustainable, high-quality outcomes. 

This is an opportunity to work on high-impact platforms that directly shape how millions of Australians experience and engage with the ABC. 

About You 

You are an established product manager with experience working on CRM, marketing automation or audience engagement platforms. You have hands-on familiarity with tools such as Braze (or similar platforms) and understand how lifecycle communications and segmentation strategies drive audience value. 

You bring strong analytical thinking and are comfortable working with data to inform prioritisation and measure impact. You can translate technical or data-related concepts into clear requirements and collaborate effectively with engineering teams. 

You are organised, detail-oriented and capable of managing multiple workstreams. You build positive working relationships across disciplines and communicate clearly with stakeholders who have varying levels of technical understanding. You're someone who is technically minded and comfortable deep in the detail, but you can also collaborate effectively with business stakeholders to drive audience strategy and outcomes. 

Most importantly, you are motivated by using digital platforms and data to strengthen meaningful audience relationships in a public service context. 

For further information on this position please contact Jamie Nedved, Recruitment Lead via email nedved.jamie@abc.net.au   

We respectfully request that Recruitment Agencies do not submit applications for this position. 

If you require recruitment or selection information in an alternative format, or need adjustments or accessibility support, please email accessibility@abc.net.au 

In line with our focus on diversity, applications are strongly encouraged from Indigenous Australians, people from a range of cultural and linguistic backgrounds, people with disability and LGBTIQA+ individuals. The ABC also aims to achieve a gender-balanced workforce 

For more information on working at the ABC visit  abc.net.au/careers

Company Type: Performance Marketing Agency

Location: Melbourne Inner Suburbs

Role: Digital Strategy Director

Channels: Paid Search, Paid Social + Programmatic

Salary Package: Confidential, competitive at Director level + performance bonus

Ready to own the performance strategy for some of Australia's most ambitious consumer brands?

This is not just another senior role. You will shape multichannel performance marketing across five high-profile client accounts, lead a team of two managers and one specialist, and be the most senior strategic voice in the room. At an agency that has built its reputation on performance excellence and genuine care for its people, this is a role worth stopping for.

You will set the strategic direction across Paid Search, Paid Social and Programmatic, drive commercial outcomes for clients spanning retail, fashion, homewares and automotive, and partner with agency leadership to raise the bar across delivery, capability and client growth.

Core Areas of Ownership

Strategy and Growth Leadership

Build multichannel growth strategies across Paid Search, Paid Social and Programmatic that connect directly to each client's commercial objectives.

Translate data, audience insights and platform signals into clear channel direction that strengthens funnel performance and long-term brand growth.

Lead annual planning and quarterly reviews, shaping strategic roadmaps that guide investment, creative direction and channel expansion.

Performance Delivery and Optimisation

Oversee delivery and optimisation across all performance channels, maintaining accuracy, efficiency and scale across five key accounts.

Hold strong technical foundations across tracking, attribution, data feeds and platform integrations.

Conduct regular audits and performance reviews to uphold best-practice account structures and execution standards.

Drive continuous innovation across testing, measurement and platform capabilities to keep clients ahead.

Team Leadership and Client Partnership

Lead two managers and one specialist, setting clear expectations, guiding development and supporting progression.

Foster a culture of high-quality craft, proactive communication and shared ownership across all performance channels.

Act as the senior strategic partner for five key clients, presenting strategies, performance narratives and growth plans with clarity and confidence.

Protect scope, manage priorities and ensure delivery standards are met across all accounts.

Experience and Background

7-9 years of hands-on performance marketing experience across Google Ads, Meta and at least one Programmatic platform.

Proven success leading multichannel strategies for mid-to-Tier 1 brands, including planning, reporting and performance optimisation.

Experience managing and developing managers, not just specialists, with a genuine investment in their growth.

Strong agency background with exposure to fast-paced delivery, cross-functional collaboration and commercial accountability.

Confident communicator who builds trust with senior stakeholders and simplifies complexity without losing substance.

What You Get in Return

Impact and Autonomy

A genuine seat at the table, with real ownership over performance strategy and the space to shape how the division evolves.

Close collaboration with agency principals on the decisions that matter, from client growth through to business direction.

Compensation and Benefits

Strong Director-level package with a performance bonus that rewards commercial impact.

$1,200 perks allowance, you actually get to choose how to spend, across fitness, learning, everyday living and charitable contributions.

Mental Health and Wellbeing

12 dedicated days off each year for your mental health, separate from and in addition to your annual leave entitlement.

A modern, pet-friendly office environment backed by confidential wellbeing support when life gets heavy.

Culture and Connection

Formally recognised as a great place to work, with a calendar of team celebrations, regular social outings and moments designed to make work feel worth it.

A people-first culture where connection is built into the rhythm of the week, not treated as an afterthought.

Growth and Development

Regular lunch and learns and company-wide masterclasses that keep you close to what is happening across the industry.

Hands-on access to major platform partners, including early programme participation and direct relationships with platform teams.

A Note on Fit

This role suits someone who is ready to lead with both confidence and humility. You know your craft deeply, you enjoy bringing others along, and you want to do your best work inside a team that cares about the same things you do.

Confidential Search: This opportunity is being managed by Touch of Talent. If you are a performance marketing leader ready to step into a senior strategy role and do some of your best work yet, we would love to hear from you. Apply now for a discreet discussion.

Company: Department of Customer Services, Open Data and Small and Family Business
Ad Text:
The Strategic Policy team within Digital Investment and Strategy, Department of Customer Services, Open Data and Small and Family Business is a multi-disciplinary unit that combines contemporary thinking and practices to deliver evidence-based and forward-looking strategic policy for Queensland Government.

As Director, Policy and Strategy you will lead a small team to provide policy advice, policy related initiatives, strategic reviews in partnership with key stakeholders across government, the community, business and industry. You will work collegiately as part of the leadership team to manage financial and human resources and undertake corporate management responsibilities effectively and efficiently.

Detailed understanding of Cabinet and Executive Government processes and decision making would be highly regarded.

As the Director, Policy and Strategy, you will:
• Provide leadership on a broad range of strategic, legislative and policy matters related to the Queensland Government's digital priorities
• Facilitate strategy development and innovative policy direction, and associated change management
• Apply robust and effective program and project management to support the delivery of strategic and policy related initiatives and their objectives
• Provide strategic advice and policy analysis across the digital and data portfolio
• Promote and support a workplace culture of equity, diversity, respect and inclusion, and proactively manage the work performance and personal conduct of your staff.

Applications to remain current for 12 months. 

Job Ad Reference: QLD/682713/26
Closing Date: Tuesday, 31 March 2026

About the Role
We are seeking a Senior Manager level Management Consultant with strong Cloud Architecture expertise to join a high-performing consulting team delivering across Federal Government Intelligence program.

This role sits at the intersection of technical depth and strategic advisory. You will work closely with senior stakeholders to shape, influence and deliver secure, scalable cloud solutions within complex, high-security environments.

You will play a key role in guiding clients through cloud strategy, architecture decisions and transformation initiatives, while ensuring solutions are aligned to both business and security requirements.

Permanent full time

NV1 Clearance mandatory

Canberra based position

2x positions available!

 

Responsibilities

Lead advisory engagements across cloud strategy, architecture and transformation programs

Engage with senior government stakeholders to influence decision-making and shape technology direction

Provide architectural oversight across cloud environments, ensuring alignment with security and compliance frameworks

Translate complex technical designs into clear, actionable insights for non-technical stakeholders

Support the design and delivery of cloud solutions across Azure and/or GCP environments

Contribute to business development activities, including proposals and client growth

Mentor and support junior consultants within the team

 

About You

Skills & Experience

Proven experience operating at Senior Manager level within a consulting environment

Strong background in cloud architecture across large-scale or complex environments

Hands-on experience with Azure or GCP, with AWS considered beneficial

Ability to understand, challenge and guide technical designs while engaging at a strategic level

Experience working within Federal Government, Police or Intlligence environments is highly regarded (not essential)

Strong stakeholder engagement skills with the ability to influence senior decision-makers

Experience delivering advisory services across cloud strategy, transformation or architecture

 

Qualifications

Relevant cloud certifications (Azure, GCP or AWS)

Active NV1 Security Clearance

Degree in a relevant discipline

 

Benefits

Motivated client – keen to interview and onboard ASAP!

Opportunity to work on high-impact national security intelligence programs

Join a growing consulting practice with a strong market reputation

Clear pathway to Director level and beyond

Flexible working arrangements within a Canberra-based team

5 weeks annual leave + additional leave entitlements

Tech and wellness perks

 

How to Apply
If you are a consulting professional who can bridge the gap between deep technical architecture and strategic advisory, we would love to hear from you. Apply now or reach out for a confidential discussion – alyssa@assemblesolutions.com.au

Company description:



Deloitte Services Pty Ltd



Job description:



Job title: Transformation Lead - ITS Automation CoE

Flexible work arrangements - work in a way that suits you best
Mentoring programs - receive support and coaching to progress your career
Impactful transformation work - help shape how organisations improve and scale their operations

We are looking for an experienced Transformation Lead professional to join our Automation Centre of Excellence in Sydney, driving process transformation and operational excellence across complex organisations. In this role, you'll work closely with business leaders to identify opportunities to standardise, optimise and reimagine key processes, using modern approaches such as process mining and data-led transformation. This is an exciting opportunity to help organisations unlock efficiency, improve performance, and build scalable processes for the future.

What will your typical day look like?

In this role, you'll be working with stakeholders across multiple teams to understand how their processes operate today and where meaningful improvements can be made. You'll combine process analysis, data insights and strong stakeholder engagement to design and deliver transformation initiatives that create measurable value.

Day to day you will be analysing current state processes, identifying opportunities for standardisation and re-engineering, and working alongside teams to implement improved ways of working. You'll also leverage process mining tools and analytics to uncover inefficiencies, validate improvements and ensure benefits are realised. Along the way, you'll help embed new processes by facilitating workshops, building clear documentation, and supporting teams through change.

About the team

You'll be joining a collaborative Automation Centre of Excellence focused on helping organisations transform how work gets done. The team brings together expertise across automation, process improvement and digital transformation to solve complex operational challenges.
Working alongside experienced transformation specialists, you'll contribute to large-scale improvement initiatives and help establish best-practice process frameworks that enable organisations to operate more efficiently and effectively.

Enough about us, let's talk about you.

You may have all or some of the following skills / experiences:

5+ years of experience in process transformation, business process improvement, or operational excellence
Experience driving process standardisation or re-engineering initiatives across complex organisations
Strong knowledge of process mapping, analysis methodologies, and frameworks such as Lean or Six Sigma
Hands-on experience with process mining tools such as Celonis, UiPath Process Mining, Signavio or similar platforms
Experience analysing process performance using data and translating insights into actionable improvements
Proven ability to engage and influence stakeholders across multiple levels of an organisation
Experience facilitating workshops and guiding teams through change and adoption of new processes
Strong analytical and problem-solving skills with a focus on delivering measurable business outcomes
Ability to lead cross-functional initiatives and manage competing priorities in a fast-paced environment
Relevant qualifications in Business, Engineering, Information Systems or similar disciplines; certifications such as Lean Six Sigma or BPM are highly regarded

Why Deloitte?

At Deloitte, we focus our energy on interesting and impactful work. We're always learning, innovating and setting the standard; making a positive difference to our clients and our society. We put coaching at the heart of what we do, helping our people grow their careers in any direction - whether it be up, moving into something new, or even moving across the world.
We embrace diversity, equity and inclusion. We have a diverse collection of people from different backgrounds, with different experiences, gender identities, abilities and thinking styles. What binds us together is a shared commitment to value everyone's perspective and to cultivate inclusion; so that our work environment is a safe space we can all belong.
We value in-person connection with our clients and our colleagues. We offer several ways for you to work flexibly so that you can serve your clients, stay connected with your team, and manage your personal priorities.
We help you live and work well. To support your personal and professional life, we offer a range of perks and benefits, including retail discounts, wellbeing leave, paid volunteering days, twelve flexible working options, market-leading parental leave and return to work support package.

Next Steps

Sound like the sort of role for you? Apply now, we'd love to hear from you!

#LI-Hybrid

By applying for this job, you'll be assessed against the Deloitte Talent Standards. We've designed these standards so that you can grow in your career, and we can provide our clients with a consistent and exceptional Deloitte employee experience globally. The preferred candidate will be subject to background screening by Deloitte or by their external third-party provider.

Senior Change Manager – Operating Model Transformation

A major mining organisation is seeking an experienced Senior Change Manager to lead the organisational change stream for a large-scale operating model transformation program.

This role sits within a strategic enterprise transformation delivering SAP S/4HANA, standardising processes, governance, and systems across multiple mining operations.

This is a high-profile leadership role responsible for driving change readiness, aligning senior stakeholders and ensuring successful adoption across operational sites and corporate functions.

The Opportunity

You will lead the change strategy across a complex transformation program that is modernising processes, data governance, supplier enablement and operational capability across the business.

Working closely with senior operational leaders and the program leadership team, you will govern the change workstream across multiple delivery partners, ensuring the organisation is fully prepared for go-live and post-implementation stabilisation.

This is a critical role focused on governance, stakeholder leadership and program alignment, rather than hands-on consulting execution.

Key Responsibilities

Change Strategy & Governance

Lead the end-to-end change strategy across the transformation program

Ensure alignment with enterprise governance and transformation frameworks

Consultant & Delivery Partner Leadership

Govern external consulting partners delivering organisational change, process design and data readiness activities

Act as the key interface between program delivery partners and operational leadership

Stakeholder & Impact Management

Lead structured Change Impact Assessments across corporate and site-based functions

Translate impacts into targeted training, communication and adoption strategies

Communications & Capability Development

Deliver structured program communications and executive reporting

Support development of training and capability uplift initiatives

Adoption & Sustainability

Define and track change adoption metrics

Identify and manage adoption risks during go-live and hypercare

Skills & Experience

Extensive Senior Change Management experience within large enterprise transformation programs

Demonstrated experience delivering change across SAP S/4HANA or large ERP implementations

Strong understanding of operations within mining, resources or heavy industry

Experience governing and managing external consulting partners

Strong executive stakeholder engagement capability

Proven experience delivering structured change frameworks including impact assessments, communications and readiness planning

An opportunity has arisen for an experienced quantitative professional to join a Credit Modelling team within a Counterparty Credit Risk (CCR) function, supporting a global commodities trading and financing business.

This role focuses on the development, enhancement and oversight of models used to measure counterparty credit exposures across trading portfolios. The successful candidate will work closely with risk, trading and credit teams to strengthen modelling frameworks and ensure alignment with regulatory expectations.

You will be part of a collaborative environment where quantitative expertise and strong stakeholder engagement are essential to delivering robust risk measurement and analysis.

Key Responsibilities
Enhance and maintain counterparty credit risk exposure models, ensuring they remain robust, accurate and aligned with internal risk standards and regulatory expectations.
Contribute to the design and development of new modelling methodologies and analytical tools used to measure counterparty exposures.
Calibrate risk factor evolution (RFE) parameters for newly traded instruments and assets not currently incorporated within existing systems.
Monitor model performance by developing processes and analytical tools to review outputs and identify areas for improvement.
Produce clear and insightful risk analysis and reporting for internal stakeholders and senior management.
Support the migration away from legacy modelling approaches, assisting with the onboarding of new products and asset classes into the primary simulation-based CCR framework.
Partner with front office trading teams and credit functions to respond to queries and provide expertise relating to CCR models and exposure calculations.

Skills and Experience
Advanced academic background in a quantitative discipline such as Mathematics, Statistics, Actuarial Science, Engineering or Computer Science.
At least 7 years' experience within a Quantitative role with exposure to the development or validation of counterparty credit risk or market risk models.
Technical proficiencies in Python, R, C++ or equivalent.

Key responsibilities

Manage operational transaction processing for General Ledger (GL) across the Commercial and marketing, selling, general and administration (MSG&A) defined accounts; ensure integrity of GL transactions processing.

Analytical support: AR Aging & Cash Allocation, Bad Debt Provision & Forecast. 

Coordinate commercial related issues: Sales reversals, Sales returns, Bad Debt, AR monitoring.  

Work with ITC to suggest improvements on process that will affect AR and Trade spend controls and reporting.

Provide development and guidance to the Senior Analyst. 

Work with the Shared Services teams to provide technical advice, guidance and any process improvement recommendations.

Work closely with the Internal Controls team to ensure that all necessary compliance tasks are being adhered to. 

Adhere to all company financial policies and procedures, and Generally Accepted Accounting Principles (US GAAP). 

Completion of month-end processes and production of key internal financial statements including profit and loss, balance sheet and cash flow. 

Completion of month end and quarterly commentary requests for Commercial and MSG&A related accounts. 

Liaise with internal and external key stakeholders to resolve queries/issues as necessary. 

Periodically assist with independent testing for Sarbanes-Oxley (Sox) certification. 

Assist on the external audit process. 

Review of Balance Sheet Reconciliations (commercial & SG&A related accounts) in accordance with KHC Financial Policy. 

Identification of potential problems and risks for management’s attention. 

Standardization of existing processes and implementation of new processes with a keen eye for utilising excel for automation 

Explain the balance sheet flux on a YOY, MOM & YTD basis for accounts assigned by the Commercial Controller and provide actionable commentary.  

Work and deliver projects as per agreed timelines that assist in insightful decision making.  

Be proficient in IFRS and SOX standards and challenge processes that do not adhere to guidelines.  

Ensure that no repeated audit findings are discovered in an internal audit assignment.  

Ensure all external audit requirements are followed up timely and accurately and no delays to any requests.  

Financial statements are prepared in accordance with company policies and US GAAP. 

Results presented to Management to assist in the conclusion of the monthly results.  

Commentary provided in a timely manner giving meaningful insights through the provision of relevant business drivers.

Timely completion of testing. 

Meet the Financial Statutory report submission on time

Ability to effectively communicate with the appropriate levels of management in both oral and written form. Communication style is clear, precise and logical.

Ad Text:
We are partnering with a large Queensland organisation undertaking a significant multi-year enterprise transformation program that will reshape its core technology ecosystem and delivery model. Over the next 12-24 months, the organisation will introduce a significant number of SaaS platforms supported by a new enterprise integration layer, while maintaining existing legacy systems and business-as-usual releases. To support this transformation, we are seeking an experienced Business Analyst to join a program stream focused on environment strategy, release management uplift and deployment capability across multiple platforms and vendors.
 
This is a unique opportunity to work within a complex transformation program where the challenge is not just implementing new platforms — but creating the operational capability required to release, integrate and manage them at scale.
 
The Opportunity:
Working alongside the Project Manager and Environment/Release SME, you will play a key role in analysing the current state environment and release processes, shaping future-state operating models, and supporting the planning and execution of capability uplift. The work will span strategy definition, operating model design and delivery planning, with strong engagement across technology teams, vendors and transformation leadership.
 
Key Responsibilities:

Analyse current environment and release management practices across multiple delivery teams
Document future-state processes and environment models supporting SaaS platforms and integration layers
Support the development of the environment strategy and release management framework
Facilitate workshops with technology teams, vendors and program stakeholders
Produce high-quality documentation including business requirements, process models and operating procedures
Support the business case and delivery planning phase for capability uplift
Identify risks, dependencies and sequencing requirements across multiple platform releases
Work closely with the Project Manager and Release SME to ensure alignment with enterprise test, deployment and change frameworks
About You:
We are looking for a Business Analyst who is comfortable operating in complex technology environments and can engage credibly with both technical and business stakeholders.
You will likely bring experience in areas such as:

Enterprise technology transformation programs
Environment management or release management analysis
Multi-platform / multi-vendor delivery environments
SaaS implementations and integration ecosystems
CI/CD pipelines, deployment practices or Dev/Test/Pre-Prod/Prod environment models
Process design and operating model definition
You'll be someone who can structure ambiguity, facilitate meaningful conversations with technical stakeholders, and produce clear artefacts that support decision-making and delivery planning. For more information please reach out to Max at mpower@sharpandcarter.com.au

Surry Hills, Sydney based with a hybrid working model

Opportunity to be part of a start up business within a large complex organisation

Develop your skills and capabilities in an innovative environment

 

The Opportunity

MILKRUN is flipping the grocery game on its head with groceries and more delivered in minutes, powered by Woolworths.  Our shelves are stocked with over 10,000 grocery items. Did we mention alcohol too? The best part? They're available for same day delivery and right now we are available in 2500+ selected suburbs across all major cities.

 

We call ourselves the 'MILKO's' - we are a small team with big ambitions. We care about every order, we keep it simple, we take ownership, we have a bias for action and we act responsibly.  We value action over deliberation, data over instinct and collaboration over hierarchy. Oh and we move FAST but we have a lot of fun whilst doing it.  




What you’ll do

This is not your standard finance role. We are a high growth business leading the charge to win in the On- Demand market. To realize this ambition, we need a detail oriented finance partner who truly understands the numbers and knows how to use them to make an impact.

You will be collaborating with multiple business units in order to provide valuable financial and commercial insights across Australia and New Zealand to support our expansion. So we’ll need exceptional communication, critical thinking and data literacy skills.

You’ll be part of a small team that operates in a large, complex organisation. The scope of this role is broad, spanning commercial, operations and FP&A activities across multiple propositions across 4 BUs, 2 countries and 2 shared services teams. Effective communication, prioritisation and organisational skills will be key to your success 

Business partner with the leadership team to analyse opportunities to grow the business including preparation of financial models for commercial and investment related decisions, benefit tracking against business cases, and risk & opportunity identification/management

Own and oversee financial performance processes, including annual planning, monthly forecasting and month-end reporting, engaging cross functionally across operations, commercial FP&A and shared service teams to ensure results are understood, outlook communicated and actions taken to improve results

Review all routine finance processes and reporting including the weekly flash, weekly accruals, liquor commission and manual claim reconciliations

Prepare management packs that articulate key drivers, performance insights and bring to life through storytelling

Drive continuous improvement to support evolving business needs and maintaining laser focus on delivering efficiency in everything we do 

Champion the MILKRUN/EcomX Finance foundations to foster vibrant team culture and continuous improvement within the finance community

 

What you’ll bring

Bachelor’s degree in commerce or accounting

CA/CPA qualification preferred

Strong modelling and analytical skills with advanced Excel / Google sheets skills

Strong data literacy skills with Big Query / SQL skills highly preferred

Strong attention to detail and good commercial sense

5 years + experience in a relevant finance role

 

What you’ll experience

A global business with endless career possibilities around every corner and across every discipline – with valuable exposure to a vast and exciting business network.

Access to Sonder.  Sonder provides free confidential 24/7 personalised financial, medical safety, psychological or physical support for team members and their families.

A progressive and competitive leave policy that gives you more space for what matters to you.  

Team discounts across our range of Woolworths Group brands you know and love and a robust rewards program that celebrates and incentivises purpose-driven work.

Opportunity to Salary Sacrifice, enabling team members to purchase selected eligible items or services from pre-tax salary.  Can be used for novated leases, superannuation contributions, Qantas Club membership, computing devices etc.

 

We are Woolworths Group   

We are Woolworths Group. 200,000+ bright minds, passionate hearts and unique perspectives connected by a shared Purpose – ‘to create better experiences together for a better tomorrow.’ It’s that Purpose that fuels our ambition to explore new ideas, make brave commitments and innovate better ways to meet the food and everyday needs of more than 24 million customers every week. 

If you’re excited to turn today’s blue sky thinking into a better tomorrow for future generations, you’ll find yourself supported and enriched in a dynamic, inclusive and empowering workplace that reflects the diverse communities we serve. With a culture of genuine care, a flexible approach to work and opportunities across the group to grow your career and make a meaningful impact, the possibilities for what we can achieve together are endless.




Everyone belongs at Woolworths Group

Diversity, equity, inclusion, and belonging are key to realising our purpose of better together for a better tomorrow. We recognise the value our team’s diversity brings to our business, customers, and communities and that teams with diverse experiences and backgrounds enrich our group and are better able to innovate and solve problems. As one of the largest employers in Australia and New Zealand, we aim to create a truly inclusive workplace where everyone feels that they belong, can be their best selves, and reach their full potential.

We encourage all candidates to apply; please let us know in your application if we can support you with any adjustments in the hiring process.

Manager, BI & Analytics (Investments Data)

Permanent, full‑time position
Flexible, hybrid working environment – Sydney or Melbourne based
Lead enterprise‑grade BI and analytics capability supporting high‑quality, data‑driven investment decisions.

At AustralianSuper, we truly care about our colleagues. We know work and life are intertwined. That’s why we support the diverse needs of everyone and have policies that enable us all to thrive and be truly flexible. We ensure diversity is celebrated for the opportunity it provides us all to learn and grow and deliver better outcomes for members.

Your New Role

Are you a BI and analytics leader who can balance strategy, delivery, and people leadership, while truly understanding the business you support?

As our Manager, BI & Analytics (Investments Data), you’ll lead a team of BI and analytics specialists delivering trusted insights that support investment decision‑making across the Fund. Reporting to the Head of Investment Data, this role provides strategic leadership, product ownership, and day‑to‑day direction for analytics solutions that must be accurate, governed, scalable, and genuinely useful.

You’ll be the face of BI & Analytics to the Investments business, working closely with senior stakeholders to understand asset classes, structures, pain points and priorities, then translating those needs into clear analytics roadmaps and high‑quality products. This is not a role for someone who wants to sit behind dashboards, it’s for a leader who enjoys engaging the business, setting direction, and building a team that can execute consistently.

You’ll also play a key role in uplifting standards and ways of working. The team has varied skillsets, so bringing consistency in how dashboards are built, governed, prioritised and delivered will be critical. With Microsoft Fabric emerging as our future platform, this role will help shape how we evolve our analytics capability over time and ensure the team is structured and skilled accordingly.

What You’ll Do

Lead and develop a high‑performing BI & Analytics team, building capability, consistency and a strong delivery culture.
Act as Product Owner for investment analytics, partnering with Investment SMEs to define priorities, manage demand and maximise business value.
Design, deliver and continuously improve BI and analytics products that support decision‑making, insight generation and risk reduction through your SME knowledge of the technical tools and Investments businesses.
Own and govern the analytics product catalogue, including roadmap development, lifecycle management and prioritisation.
Establish and maintain strong governance, quality standards, metadata and controls to ensure analytics outputs are trusted and consistent.
Engage and influence senior leaders by clearly communicating insights, recommendations and the “why” behind the data.

What You’ll Need

Proven experience leading and developing BI & Analytics teams in complex environments.
Advanced hands‑on expertise in Power BI and SQL, with strong understanding of data modelling and analytics best practice.
Experience working within the Microsoft analytics ecosystem, including Azure, Power Platform and ideally Microsoft Fabric. 
Experience supporting and truly understanding investment data environments (including in-depth knowledge of an Investments business).
Strong delivery management capability, including backlog, demand and pipeline management using Agile or iterative approaches.
Ability to engage credibly with senior stakeholders, challenge assumptions, and translate business needs into analytics solutions.
Demonstrated curiosity and innovation mindset - always looking for better ways to use data to solve business problems.
Experience establishing analytics governance, standards, and quality controls.
Strong communication skills, with the ability to explain complex insights clearly to technical and non‑technical audiences.

This is an opportunity to join a team driving genuine, enterprise-wide transformation.

Role Details:

You will work at the intersection of strategy, operations, and technology, helping Visy unlock value from automation, RPA and AI across multiple business units. The role has strong executive visibility and direct impact on our digital roadmap and operational efficiency.

You will accelerate our digital and AI transformation agenda across the business. With a growing portfolio of automation and AI initiatives spanning Finance, Logistics, Procurement, Shared Services and other core functions, you will lead the expansion of our AI Centre of Excellence.

You will be a critical enabler of our transformation strategy, identifying opportunities, shaping business cases, designing future‑state processes, and ensuring AI and automation initiatives deliver measurable business value.

What you’ll do:

In this strategic and hands-on role, you will:

Lead structured process discovery workshops to identify RPA, automation, AI and GenAI opportunities across business units.
Conduct end-to-end assessments (As‑Is / To‑Be, feasibility, value and ROI analysis) for automation and AI use cases.
Define and document business requirements, user stories and future‑state process models aligned to Visy’s digital strategy.
Manage and prioritise the automation & AI pipeline, ensuring clear intake, triage, governance and tracking.
Partner closely with technical and delivery teams to shape solution scope and support successful implementation.
Influence and manage stakeholders across business and IT, supporting change management and adoption.

What you bring:

We’re looking for a strategic Lead BA who can operate comfortably in both detail and big picture:

10–12 years’ experience in business analysis, transformation or process re‑engineering roles.
Significant experience across automation / RPA / AI / digital transformation programs in complex environments.
Strong track record in process mapping (As‑Is / To‑Be), opportunity qualification and business case development.
Proven stakeholder management skills, including working with senior business and technology leaders.
Excellent facilitation, documentation and presentation skills, with the ability to produce executive‑ready materials.
Experience with tools such as RPA platforms and AI / GenAI technologies (e.g. Microsoft Copilot, Azure OpenAI) is highly regarded.

 What you’ll love when you join us:  

Competitive salary and benefits
High‑impact digital transformation work
Supportive, collaborative team
Strong career growth opportunities
Lifestyle and purchased leave options
Discounts with RSEA, Harvey Norman, BMW, Avis and more

Ready to make a difference? Apply now and be a key player in shaping our future!  

About Visy

Visy is powering the circular economy, closing the loop between packaging, remanufacturing and recycling.  Over 75 years, we've grown to 7,000 employees and 150 global operations – predominantly in Australia and New Zealand.  

We believe materials are not recycled until they’ve been made into new products. We collect and sort recyclable materials like paper, cardboard, glass, plastics and metals from households and businesses, and locally remanufacture them back into new, sustainable packaging. From cardboard boxes, water bottles and jam jars to food and beverage cans, we are using recycled content to power Australia and New Zealand’s food and beverage packaging industry. 

Ready to make a difference? Apply now and be a key player in shaping our future!
